methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

path: For Block edges: decode proof, validate assertion hashes, check endHistoryRoot
EdgeChallengeManagerLib.createLayerZeroEdge (type checks, common checks, add to store)
transformations: Assertion state data decoded from args.proof
Machine hashes computed via oneStepProofEntry.getMachineHash
startHistoryRoot computed from single-leaf Merkle tree of startState
Edge ID computed as keccak256(mutualId, endHistoryRoot)
Mutual ID computed as keccak256(level, originId, startHeight, startHistoryRoot, endHeight)
data_flows.trust_changes: User-supplied assertion data is validated against on-chain assertion chain; proof data trusted after Merkle verification
validation_points: Assertion pending and has sibling checks
Merkle inclusion proof for endState in endHistoryRoot
Merkle prefix proof from start to end
For non-Block: hasLengthOneRival check on claim, level progression check
exposure_risks: Edge data is publicly visible in storage
manipulation_risks: Race condition in firstRivals: the order of edge creation determines which stake stays in contract vs goes to excessStakeReceiver
Adversary can front-run honest edge creation to become the 'first' edge

path: Check edge is Pending and has rival
Create lower and upper child edges
transformations: mandatoryBisectionHeight computed from start/end heights using XOR and MSB operations
Lower child: [startHeight, middleHeight] with startHistoryRoot to bisectionHistoryRoot
Upper child: [middleHeight, endHeight] with bisectionHistoryRoot to endHistoryRoot
data_flows.trust_changes: Bisection history root trusted after prefix proof verification
validation_points: Edge must have rival (prevents unnecessary bisection)
Children already set check (prevents double bisection)
Lower child may already exist (merge move)
manipulation_risks: Adversary controls bisection timing but height is deterministic
Lower child may pre-exist from rival bisection, affecting rival tracking

path: Leaf edges accumulate timeUnrivaled based on block.number - createdAtBlock
updateTimerCacheByChildren propagates min(lowerChild, upperChild) cache to parent
updateTimerCacheByClaim propagates claiming edge's cache to claimed edge (cross-level)
transformations: timeUnrivaled: block.number - createdAtBlock (if unrivaled), or firstRivalCreatedAtBlock - createdAtBlock (if rivaled)
timeUnrivaledTotal: timeUnrivaled + min(children caches)
Timer cache capped at uint64.max
Timer cache only increases (monotonic)
data_flows.trust_changes: Timer values derived from block.number which is trusted on L1
validation_points: Only one rival per mutual group can be confirmed
Assertion unrivaled blocks added for first-child block edges
manipulation_risks: Adversary can delay calling updateTimerCacheByChildren for honest edges, but honest party can call it themselves
Timer cache monotonicity means an adversary cannot decrease a timer once set

path: getPrevAssertionHash traverses edge levels via firstRivals to find Block-level origin
Calculate machineStep by traversing levels with stepSize multiplication
Execute one step proof via oneStepProofEntry.proveOneStep
Set edge as confirmed with max timer cache
transformations: machineStep = SmallStep.startHeight + sum(stepSize * BigStep[i].startHeight)
stepSize grows by bigStepHeight at each level
afterHash produced by oneStepProofEntry.proveOneStep
data_flows.trust_changes: One step proof entry contract is fully trusted; execution context from prevConfig is validated against assertion chain
validation_points: Merkle inclusion proofs for before and after states
manipulation_risks: machineStep calculation traverses via firstRivals which returns the SECOND edge created; all rivals share same startHeight so this is safe
If oneStepProofEntry has a bug, incorrect edges could be confirmed

path: Validate staker is staked with sufficient amount
Validate prev assertion exists and staker can move
RollupCore.createNewAssertion: validate config, states, inbox position
If not first child: transfer requiredStake to loserStakeEscrow
transformations: assertionHash = keccak256(parentAssertionHash, afterState.hash(), inboxAcc)
configHash = keccak256(wasmModuleRoot, requiredStake, challengeManager, confirmPeriodBlocks, nextInboxPosition)
nextInboxPosition adjusted if no new messages (currentInboxPosition + 1)
data_flows.trust_changes: User-supplied assertion data validated against on-chain state (bridge, inbox, prev assertion)
validation_points: Staker is active and has sufficient stake
Machine status constraints (FINISHED or ERRORED after, FINISHED before)
Minimum assertion period for non-overflow assertions
manipulation_risks: If stakeToken is fee-on-transfer, receiveTokens receives less than expected
Non-first-child stake transfer may drain contract if many branches created
Excess stake beyond requiredStake may become stuck (acknowledged in comments)

path: Validate assertion exists and is pending
If prev has second child: verify winning edge from challengeManager
transformations: assertionHash validated as keccak256(parentAssertionHash, confirmState.hash(), inboxAcc)
blockHash and sendRoot extracted from confirmState for outbox update
data_flows.trust_changes: winningEdge data from challengeManager is trusted if prevConfig.challengeManager is validated against configHash
validation_points: Winning edge confirmed and grace period passed
manipulation_risks: challengeManager address in prevConfig is user-supplied but validated against configHash
Grace period ensures challenge result is observable before assertion confirmation

path: edge.setRefunded() - checks confirmed, layer zero, not already refunded
data_flows.trust_changes: No access control - anyone can trigger refund but tokens always go to edge.staker
validation_points: Edge must be layer zero (has claimId and staker)
Edge must not already be refunded
manipulation_risks: If the confirmed edge was NOT the first edge created for its mutual group, its stake was sent to excessStakeReceiver, but the contract still holds the first edge's stake amount - sufficient for exactly one refund per mutual group
If stakeToken is fee-on-transfer, edge.staker receives less than stakeAmounts[level]

core_rules: Each mutual ID group (edges sharing same start parameters and end height) can have at most one confirmed edge
Timer caches are monotonically non-decreasing and capped at uint64.max
Edges are never deleted from storage, only created and potentially confirmed
Layer zero edges require stake deposits; only one stake per mutual group is retained in the contract
Bisection heights are deterministic to ensure rival edges bisect at the same points
Only SmallStep length-1 edges can be confirmed via one-step proof
Only layer zero edges can be confirmed by time
Challenge hierarchy: Block → BigStep(s) → SmallStep, each level claims a length-1 edge in the level below
Assertions can only be confirmed after deadline passes and winning edge exists for disputed assertions
economic_incentives: Stakers must deposit tokens to create layer zero edges, disincentivizing spam
First edge's stake retained in contract for refund; subsequent rivals' stakes sent to excessStakeReceiver
Honest validator is expected to be reimbursed via excessStakeReceiver (out-of-band mechanism)
Validators stake tokens to create assertions; non-first-child stakes sent to loserStakeEscrow
Only winning assertion's staker effectively retains their stake
Challenge period must be long enough to cover censorship resistance + offchain computation time
governance_mechanisms: Validator whitelist can be disabled via removeWhitelistAfterFork (chain ID change) or removeWhitelistAfterValidatorAfk (201600 blocks inactivity)
anyTrustFastConfirmer can bypass normal confirmation process (intended for AnyTrust chains)
Security council / admin can pause the contract via PausableUpgradeable

edge_cases.scenario: Fee-on-transfer stake token used in EdgeChallengeManager
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Contract receives exact stakeAmounts[level] on edge creation and refunds exact stakeAmounts[level]
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Contract receives less than stakeAmounts[level] due to transfer fee, but refundStake attempts to transfer the full stakeAmounts[level], potentially causing insufficient balance reverts for some refunds
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If stakeToken charges fees, the contract balance will be less than expected, and some stakers may not be able to claim refunds. Unlikely if standard ERC20 is used as intended, but no explicit defense.

edge_cases.scenario: Losing staker calls returnOldDeposit after their assertion's child is created
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Losing staker should not receive their full stake back since requiredStake was sent to loserStakeEscrow
edge_cases.actual_behavior: withdrawStaker adds full amountStaked to withdrawableFunds, but the contract may lack tokens because requiredStake was already transferred to loserStakeEscrow. The withdrawStakerFunds call will revert on safeTransfer, but _withdrawableFunds and totalWithdrawableFunds remain inflated.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Creates phantom withdrawable balances in accounting. Losing stakers cannot actually withdraw (safeTransfer fails), but totalWithdrawableFunds is permanently inflated, creating inconsistent accounting state.

edge_cases.scenario: Multiple non-first-child assertions created from same predecessor
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Each non-first-child creation sends requiredStake to loserStakeEscrow from contract balance
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Each creation deducts requiredStake from rollup contract balance. If many branches created, contract may not have sufficient balance for both loserStakeEscrow transfers and honest staker withdrawals.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Requires significant capital to create many branches. Each branch requires a staker with requiredStake deposited. The contract receives each staker's deposit and immediately sends requiredStake to escrow, so balance should net to one requiredStake per predecessor.

edge_cases.scenario: getPrevAssertionHash called on edge where lower-level firstRivals is UNRIVALED
edge_cases.actual_behavior: If firstRivals[originId] returns UNRIVALED hash, get(store, UNRIVALED) reverts with EdgeNotExists. However, this scenario should be unreachable because higher-level edges can only exist when the lower-level claim has rivals.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: No practical exploitation path due to edge creation constraints, but the function lacks a graceful error message for this case.

edge_cases.scenario: Zero stakeAmounts for some levels with non-zero stakeToken
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Edges at those levels are created without stake
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Code correctly handles this: if sa == 0, no transfer occurs. However, edges at those levels can be created for free, removing economic disincentive for spam.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If a level has zero stake, adversaries can spam edges at that level without cost, potentially causing DoS on honest validators who must respond.

edge_cases.scenario: Edge confirmed by one-step-proof has different ordering of setConfirmedRival and setConfirmed vs confirmEdgeByTime
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Both paths should atomically confirm the edge and record it as the confirmed rival
edge_cases.actual_behavior: confirmEdgeByOneStepProof calls setConfirmedRival then setConfirmed, while confirmEdgeByTime calls setConfirmed then setConfirmedRival. Both are atomic within the transaction so neither ordering creates a vulnerability.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: No exploitation, but code inconsistency could mask future bugs if either function is modified.

edge_cases.scenario: Block edge creation when assertion predecessor has more than 2 children
edge_cases.expected_behavior: childCreated silently ignores 3rd+ children (no secondChildBlock update)
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Works correctly since only firstChildBlock and secondChildBlock are tracked. 3rd+ children function normally as non-first children.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: None - the protocol handles arbitrary numbers of children correctly via the isFirstChild flag.

edge_cases.scenario: Honest validator's edge is the 2nd created (stake goes to excessStakeReceiver) but it gets confirmed
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Confirmed edge staker should receive refund
edge_cases.actual_behavior: refundStake transfers stakeAmounts[level] from the contract. Since the 1st edge's stake is in the contract (regardless of who created it), the refund succeeds. The economic loss to the honest validator is their stake going to excessStakeReceiver initially, but the refund comes from the adversary's retained stake.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: The protocol ensures one stake per mutual group is retained. The honest validator may have paid their stake to excessStakeReceiver, but receives the equivalent amount back from the retained stake. Net result is correct.

edge_cases.scenario: confirmEdgeByTime called with arbitrary claimStateData for non-block-level or non-first-child edge
edge_cases.actual_behavior: For non-block-level edges or non-first-child claims, assertionBlocks remains 0 and claimStateData is not validated. Correct behavior - no impact from garbage data.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: None - unused data cannot affect outcome.

edge_cases.scenario: VALIDATOR_AFK_BLOCKS constant on a chain with non-12-second block time
edge_cases.expected_behavior: 28 days of inactivity before whitelist removal
edge_cases.actual_behavior: 201600 blocks at non-12-second block time gives different calendar time. On a 2-second block chain, this would be ~4.67 days instead of 28 days.
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: On chains with faster block times, the validator whitelist could be removed much sooner than intended, allowing anyone to participate in the rollup validation prematurely.

assumption_violations: stakeToken is assumed to be a standard ERC20 without fee-on-transfer, rebasing, or callback mechanisms. Using a non-standard token breaks refund accounting in both EdgeChallengeManager and RollupUserLogic.
block.number is assumed to monotonically increase at a predictable rate. On L2 chains or after hard forks, block timing assumptions may break.
The one step proof entry contract is assumed to be correct and non-malicious. A compromised or buggy oneStepProofEntry could confirm invalid edges.
The bridge and assertion chain contracts are assumed to be correct. Incorrect sequencerInboxAccs or assertion data would break the challenge integrity.
It's assumed that honest validators will actively update timer caches and respond to challenges within the challenge period. If they don't, adversary edges can win by time.
game_theory_risks: An adversary can create many rival edges at each level, forcing the honest party to respond to each via bisection. The stake requirement limits this but each response costs gas.

Timer inheritance means an adversary who delays their challenges (creating rivals late) gives honest edges more unrivaled time. The adversary cannot claw back time once the honest edge has accumulated it.

The excessStakeReceiver mechanism means the first edge creator's stake is retained while later ones go to excess. A sequencer could front-run honest validators to ensure the adversary's stake is retained and the honest validator's goes to excess, though this doesn't affect correctness since refunds come from the retained stake.
Creating assertions on multiple branches (fork attacks) requires proportional capital (requiredStake per branch) which limits the economic viability of such attacks.
The challenge grace period prevents confirmation immediately after a winning edge is found, giving time for the community to observe and respond to potentially incorrect outcomes.
integration_risks: The assertionChain interface is critical - if validateAssertionHash or validateConfig are compromised, invalid edges or assertions could be created.
oneStepProofEntry executes arbitrary computation to produce afterHash. A vulnerability in the proof system could allow false confirmations.
bridge.sequencerInboxAccs is trusted for inbox accumulator values. If the bridge is compromised, assertion validity checks break.
The outbox.updateSendRoot call in confirmAssertionInternal is a trusted external call that updates L2-to-L1 message roots.

safeTransferFrom/safeTransfer for stakeToken are external calls at end of functions. If token has reentrancy vectors (ERC-777), the onlyValidator modifier and isStaked checks provide some protection but no explicit reentrancy guard exists.
calculation_errors: machineStep calculation in confirmEdgeByOneStepProof accumulates startHeight * stepSize across BigStep levels. For very large heights (e.g., all at 2^26) and many levels, the intermediate products could be very large but won't overflow uint256 given reasonable parameters (total < 2^256).
mandatoryBisectionHeight uses XOR and MSB to find deterministic bisection points. The formula ((end - 1) & mask) correctly identifies the highest power-of-2-aligned boundary between start and end.

timeUnrivaled calculation: when firstRivalCreatedAtBlock == edgeCreatedAtBlock, returns 0. This means edges created in the same block as their rival get zero unrivaled time, which is correct behavior.

assertionBlocks calculation: getSecondChildCreationBlock - getFirstChildCreationBlock. Both are uint64, subtraction is safe because secondChildBlock >= firstChildBlock (second child always created after or at same time as first).

token_semantics.notes: Used as IERC20 with SafeERC20. Code assumes no fee-on-transfer: transfers exact stakeAmounts[level] and refunds same amount. Fee-on-transfer tokens would cause accounting mismatch. The contract does NOT check received amounts.

Same stake token used in rollup. SafeERC20 used. Token received AFTER state changes in newStakeOnNewAssertion (CEI not strictly followed but atomicity provides safety). Transfer to loserStakeEscrow occurs from contract balance; if contract balance is insufficient due to prior transfers, safeTransfer reverts.

invariants.name: single_confirmed_rival
invariants.description: For each mutualId, at most one edge can be confirmed. Enforced by setConfirmedRival which reverts if confirmedRivals[mutualId] is already set.

invariants.name: timer_cache_monotonicity
invariants.description: Timer cache values only increase. updateTimerCache only writes if newValue > currentAccuTimer. Capped at uint64.max.

invariants.name: edge_immutability
invariants.description: Edges are never deleted. Core fields (originId, startHistoryRoot, startHeight, endHistoryRoot, endHeight, level) never change after creation. Only status, children, refunded, confirmedAtBlock, and totalTimeUnrivaledCache can be updated.

invariants.name: single_bisection
invariants.description: setChildren reverts if children are already set, preventing double bisection.

invariants.name: single_refund
invariants.description: setRefunded reverts if already refunded. Only confirmed layer zero edges can be refunded.

invariants.name: stake_balance_conservation
invariants.description: For each mutual group, exactly one stakeAmounts[level] is retained in the contract (from the first edge). All subsequent rivals' stakes go to excessStakeReceiver. At most one refund per mutual group is possible.

invariants.name: deterministic_bisection_height
invariants.description: mandatoryBisectionHeight is a pure function of start and end heights, ensuring rival edges bisect at identical points, which is critical for the merge-move optimization.

invariants.name: level_progression
invariants.description: Levels progress strictly: Block(0) → BigStep(1..N) → SmallStep(N+1). Layer zero edges at level L+1 claim length-1 rivaled edges at level L. nextEdgeLevel enforces this.
